current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.

recent_results.description: Keep the improved JumpReLU K=128 anchor fixed and continue the LR sweep upward to 3.2e-3 as the next single-parameter refinement.
Keep the improved JumpReLU K=128 anchor fixed and continue the LR sweep upward to 3.2e-3 as the next single-parameter refinement. full
Hold the improved JumpReLU K=128 setup fixed at LR=1.6e-3 and start the auxk sweep by setting AUXK_ALPHA=0 to test whether pure reconstruction training beats the current anchor.

recent_round_summaries.hypothesis: The current JumpReLU K=128 baseline may benefit from more aggressive signum updates, and increasing LR to 1.6e-3 could improve optimization enough to beat both the failed 4e-4 proxy and the standing 0.1888 full-FVU anchor.
JumpReLU at K=128 may still be under-updating relative to its new 1.6e-3 optimum, and increasing the signum learning rate to 3.2e-3 could further reduce FVU beyond the current 0.1724 full frontier.
JumpReLU at K=128 may be paying unnecessary reconstruction cost to the auxiliary dead-feature objective, and removing auxk pressure could lower primary FVU below the current 0.1724 full frontier.

gated.design_hypothesis: A gated SAE that decouples latent support selection from reconstruction magnitude can beat the current JumpReLU anchor at K=128 by learning a separate sigmoid routing branch while staying initialization-close to plain ReLU top-k.
next_steps: If gated shows better proxy FVU at acceptable throughput, stabilize the family by exposing and tuning gate temperature/init strength in a follow-up.
If gated quality is promising but throughput regresses, keep the family alive and do a performance-focused follow-up rather than discarding the architecture.
If gated fails to improve proxy FVU, move on to a different routing-focused family instead of more JumpReLU-local sweeps.

recent_performance_findings: round 3: suspected performance regression for jumprelu (tps=16601.650675, baseline_ratio=0.200000, termination=throughput_too_low)
next_move_guidance: If AUXK_ALPHA=0 improves proxy FVU, promote to full and then refine auxk near zero, such as 0.01, on the same K=128 anchor.
If AUXK_ALPHA=0 is worse, restore auxk and test AUXK_ALPHA=0.01 next to probe whether a smaller but nonzero auxiliary weight is optimal.
If auxk changes do not improve quality, keep K=128 fixed and start expansion-factor sweeps, beginning with EXPANSION_FACTOR=16.
